rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BROOKS CARROLL VS. MARTIN L. ENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Award Of Arbitration James H Fleming Esq. Rendered On May 4, 2011
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 31, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL CARROLL Ntc Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Award Of Arbitration James H Fleming Esq. Rendered On May 4, 2011 is Granted. The hearing was continued from November 4, 2011 to permit further filings on Mr. Eng's allegations the arbitrator was biased against him. The court has reviewed the declaration of the arbitrator which shall be filed and all papers filed by the parties since the prior hearing. There are no grounds to deny confirmation of the award. The allegations of bias by the arbitrator are unfounded. There is no evidence of bias against Mr. Eng. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL L. BALSAM VS. DSG DIRECT INC., A FLORIDA CORPOATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Tuesday Jan.31, 2012 Line 1, - PLAINTIFF DANIEL BALSAM's Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions, Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Oiton For Leave To File X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 31, 2012, Line 2, CROSS-COMPLAINANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is Granted. While State Fund should have brought the motion earlier, the strong policy favoring amendments, the absence of a strong showing of bad faith by State Fund and the fact that any prejudice to plaintiff can be remedied by a trial continuance requires that the motion be granted. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM A. WINEBERG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 31, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM WINEBERG Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees is Denied. Plaintiff is not entitled to an award of fees under either section 1021.5 or 1988(b). Plaintiff has not shown any of the four elements for an award of fees under section 1021.5. Plaintiff cannot recover fees under section 1988 because he did not obtain a judgment on the merits or a court decree that materially altered the relationship of the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. MARTIN R GUAJARDO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Criminal Investigation; Declarations Of Steven Gruel And Kristen E. Caverly In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 31, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER STENDER AND IMMIGRATION PRACTICE GROUP, P.C.'S Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Criminal Investigation IS Denied. None of the applicable factors weigh in favor of defendants' request for an open-ended stay of all pre-trial proceedings or alternative request for an open-ended stay of all party discovery. While it may be appropriate to stay specified activities such as Mr. Stender's deposition or to appoint a discovery referee, the parties have not formally requested such relief and, more importantly, do not appear to have met and conferred regarding any discovery plan designed to address Mr. Stender's intention to invoke the Fifth Amendment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/HK

rulings.Case Title: ALI KIA SHABAHANGI et al VS. NADER SHABAHANGI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 31, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI AND AGESONG INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to all claims alleged by all plaintiffs. As to plaintiff Syuergy, leave is granted to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that it has the legal capacity to sue. As to the individual plaintiffs, leave to amend is granted to allege ultimate facts supporting each cause of action alleged or any or other cause of action that they may wish to allege in an amended complaint. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 31, 2012, Line 20, PLAINTIFF DB SERVICING CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Response To Discovery, Order For The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Asserted And Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions IS Off calendar. The entire action was dismissed on January 17, 2012. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: ALAN DUANE FOSTER VS. GEORGE VALVERDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice To Grant Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 31, 2012, Line 21, PETITIONER ALAN DUANE FOSTER Notice To Grant Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus IS Denied. The weight of the evidence supports the finding of Hearing Officer Nguyen that Officer Conceicao read the Vehicle Code 23612(a) admonition to Mr. Foster. (Reporter's Transcript 8:13-17; 9:9-10:5). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 31, 2012, Line 22, DEFENDANT LAUREEN PARAGUYA DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Continued to February 7, 2012 to enable Mr. Butler to provide copies of his opposition papers to department 302 in compliance with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. Butler must deliver copies of his opposition papers to department 302 no later than 4pm January 31 and those papers must be accompanied with a note or cover letter stating the new hearing date of February 7, 2012. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE NORTON VS. HERTZ VEHICLES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Change Venue; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 31, 2012, Line 23, DEFENDANT CAROLIN MAPLES' MOTION To Change Venue IS Denied. At this stage of the case, the only ground for change of venue that appears to be potentially applicable venue is proper in San Francisco County since the complaint alleges that Hertz operates and does business in San Francisco. Transfer on the basis of the convenience of witnesses is premature because Ms. Maples has not filed her answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/HK
